review_title: 249,576 Files Are Safe from Natural Diaster
description: Since it runs in the background and I don't need to do anything with it - my experience is awesome! I love seeing the reports pop on the screen saying my system just backed up XXXXXXX number of files. And at any time, I can open the software and see the files and view my backup on the Cloud. I am using Carbonite Safe CE 22.2 -- I have clients that say they have a backup to Google or other service, and quite frankly they don't understand the complexity they will go through to try to retrieve their data. I highly recommend Carbonite not only for business but also personal home computers.
author: Susan A.
pros: Peace of mind. At any point in time, I know if my hard drive system were to be compromised, by disaster or stolen or other calamity--I could be back in business with all documents and files uploaded into a new computer hard drive system. I have stored in my system at this moment in time 249,577 files - work from over 10 years is compiled here for my marketing clients. My invoices and accounting are backed up. My IRS files are backed up.
cons: As I was recommended to Carbonite by my IT guy, he is the one installed the software and also uploaded all of the docs and files when I purchase new hardware. So if I were to lose my data I would need to figure out how to do it and/or pay him to help me. I trust experts when it comes to my data and security, so this is not a huge issue for me.

description: Overall I think it's a good software that I especially liked because as our organization is growing nationally, it grows with us.
author: Sunaina B.
pros: It was helpful for when system glitches or internet outages occur to assist with data recovery.
cons: The keep having an increase in price and have gotten no new features.

description: The transfer speed is absurd and caused me a lot of embarrassment. I had a client have an 8tb array fail. This took over 2 weeks to completely recovery via carbonite. They have a 1gb/s fiber connection.
author: Donnie P.
pros: There's not much. They market heavily, so customers recognize and trust the name, though I do believe that they shouldn't.
cons: The service portal is abysmal, slow, and barely functional. The customer support is terrible. Transfer speed is terrible and the app crashes frequently

review_title: Carbonite Functions Very Well For File Level Backups!
description: Overall the product has worked very well for us.
author: Tony M.
pros: Easy to setup and use for file backups and recovery.
cons: Sometimes support is less than we expect in terms of helpfulness.

description: Awesome. I ave had to use the backups to recover failed drives, and ransomed disks.
author: Randy W.
pros: The software is easy to setup. It allows the individual to set up different backups. Incremental (daily), full (weekly or daily). Support is great and very helpful. You can encrypt the backups. Carbonite checks the backups on a continuous basis for ransomware.
cons: By default it selects the folders for you. This is OK, but not all folders need backed up. It is easy to unselect the folders.

description: If Carbonite changed those two factors I mentioned as cons, I would feel way more confident recommending them to friends because I do like the affordable plan and how easy it is to backup files compared to competitors. And like I mentioned, I also like how they will back up your external hard drive which a competitor I was looking at does not do.
author: Sarah S.
pros: As a backstory, I was talking to our tech advisor about our need for backing up our data. While I ensure that I back up my data through my external hard drive, I also know that my external hard drive can go kaput as all of them do eventually. So I mentioned to our tech guy that I feel like we need a cloud storage but I wasn't too fond of Dropbox or Google Drive. He had similar sentiments and recommended Carbonite. He said he uses it with a number of clients with good results. So I took the plunge with their Black Friday deal that included backup for my computer and external hardrive. Pros: I liked that it backed up all of my data itself and that I didn't have to do it manually. The only thing I need to do manually is for my future data and the process is SUPER simple even for the tech-challenged. I also liked that there was the mid-tier option (the one I chose) that backs up your external hard drive. You get unlimited storage for the tier I chose which is not true of Dropbox, OneDrive, GoogleDrive, and others.
cons: I don't like that I can't easily access my data to see with my own eyes that my data is backed up which is very different than OneDrive, GoogleDrive, and Dropbox that I can pull up at any time my backup files. I can't tell how good Carbonite is at backing up the files until something goes wrong which makes me nervous. I don't like that if my external hard drive isn't connected for 30 days, the data is wiped out. What if I'm taking a break from my technology and don't have it hooked up? What if I'm using a different computer at the time because my original laptop and backup hard drive are getting repaired? That made me feel uneasy.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I have been using Carbonite for Years and the Customer Service is 5 star and the software will auto backup all your data all day long just set it and forget it.
author: Darell B.
pros: I have used Carbonite for a long time and recommend to all my business customers because it is the most reliable backup on the market and easy to use.
cons: The pricing is a little expensive but it is a Great Product

description: Carbonite has been a great application for backing up computers. We started with mostly desktops and have transitioned to mostly mobile computers without issue.
author: Scott M.
pros: Provides seemless backup for team of computers
cons: It is based on amount of storage and we continually have to adjust our plan.

review_title: No-Frills Backup Solution To Give You Peace Of Mind
description: Carbonite consistently backs-up our end-user Apple and Windows machines– which is exactly what we need it to do.
author: Luke ..
pros: Recent software and portal updates have greatly improved the consistency of our backups as well as the ease of administration.
cons: We recently encountered a problem that broke a large portion of our backups. Support was very helpful and responsive, but it took about a month for a patch to be released to

description: Great service, support, and very easy to deploy
author: Sean H.
pros: Fantastic for cost effective server/endpoint/365 backups
cons: Can be pricy when you have lots of storage on office 365

description: Overall good. I was happy with the service.
author: Terry P.
pros: Top quality product, retrieval was made easy enough.
cons: A bit clunky, old interface, a bit expensive.

description: If a document was accidently deleted, you could go into your account online and retrieve it for a short period of time.
author: Cindy B.
pros: Once you have it installed on your computer/laptop, you don't have to worry about it, the back up is automatic. Besides my work computer, I have also purchased it for my personal computer because it is so convenient, the auto backup and the availability of your files from anywhere.
cons: So far our company has been very happy with this safety net.

review_title: After being a customer for 15 years, service fails constantly - ultimately a waste of time and money
description: After being a customer for fifteen years, I am thoroughly disappointed with Carbonite's product and their complete unwillingness to refund anything for the time my PC spent not being backed up last year.
author: Tim H.
pros: It backs up my files some of the time. It's relatively easy to get your files back, the files it actually backs up.
cons: * It rarely backs up my files. The backup is usually more than 60 days old.* Service fails constantly* A tool that is supposed to save me time and money is now costing me hours and hours of troubleshooting and support calls

author: Catherine D.
pros: Easy to use and reliable. Bought it for work and then got it for my home computers as well
cons: Recovering one or two files at a time was not intuitive and took a bit of time to sort out.

description: I hav ebeen using Carbonite for about 5 years.
author: Gregory W.
pros: Carbonite was one of the first cloud backup services available. For an annual fee (which can be renewed for up to three years at a time), you can have all your files uploaded to a secure server. All files are encrypted and stored safely until you need them again. Carbonite constantly checks your system for new files and updates the backup as needed. Carbonite also has an option to back up your entire system--not just document files. The best selling point is that your files are encrypted before they leave your system.
cons: Carbonite is a bit on the expensive side, when you consider that there are less expensive options available for simple (unencrypted) cloud services. Also, restoring a backup from Carbonite can take many days because of the encryption/decryption process. Unless you absolutely need a system backup, use another service for cloud storage.

description: The service that carbonite provides is invaluable. If you don't have your computer files backed up in a remote location you really need to. We have computers "die" all the time and without carbonite (or a similar service) we would be in danger of losing years worth of work.
author: Adam M.
pros: I like how easy it is to setup Carbonite. You download the software then simply tell it what files/folders you want backed up. Then you tell it how often to back up. It is easy to setup and after it is setup, you'll forget its there...until your computer dies and you need to restore the files. Luckily, having carbonite means that if/when your computer dies, you won't lose your valuable files. Their customer support is really good. I was having trouble restoring files and was able to get a support agent on the phone within 10 minutes and within another 5-10 minutes we were good to go.
cons: The carbonite website is pretty slow. I am restoring about 20gb of files now and it is going super slow. It is saying it'll take 16hrs to download 20gb. That's insanely slow. I know it is not our internet because I transfer large files a lot and it doesn't take long with our internet speed in the office.

review_title: Good Program but can get Pricey
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It offers an additional back up source for me at a reasonable cost. I am a belt and suspenders type of person so I want multiple back ups of everything I do. This provides me with an easy way to have an extra backup source.
cons: I looked into using this program as my main back up software but when you start looking at the server version it can get expensive in a hurry.

review_title: I do not have enough space to tell you of how this software fails.
description: I can not offer any user a benefit to using this company for backing up critical user documents, pictures and data. My recommendation is to find something different.
author: James E.
pros: It was installed and configured by the previous admin, that's it.Honestly there is nothing positive about this software.
cons: Horrible user setup, Bad server support, Software required daily administration to keep users up to date. Customer service was terrible. No matter what the reason for contact tech support they could not resolve even basic errors. Server support would bloat our data backup and would not release expired backups. Unable to restore ANY user files no matter the user backup size. The software/ company cost me over 300gb of lost data due to failed server backups. The final straw was when they had a security breech and sent all users an email "click on this link to reset your password" What happened, as I was told a senior executive decided to blow out ALL customer access passwords and send this email to reset. Oh, and let's not forget that this little blowup allowed you to use the SAME password that just got dumped.

review_title: Always getting E-mails that backup did not occur!
description: If you save a lot to your computer, I would suggest this product.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: If I were in need, I think this back up could be a lifesaver. Thankfully, I have never been in that situation. I also like that it is automatic.
cons: I get e-mails very frequently that my backups are not working. It is frustrating to pay for something that is not actually serving its' purpose. Also was unaware I set up an auto-renew. Went to cancel, and found out I just recently paid for a year.

review_title: Customer service couldn't fix my issue and wouldn't refund my 3-year prepaid subscription
author: Ross M.
pros: I've used Cabonite in the past and had a good experience. It was a reliable way to backup my laptop and served as a way to have remote access to my documents, files and pictures.
cons: Carbonite caused my MacBook Pro to constantly run which made it get very hot to the touch. After a few weeks of identifying the issue, I uninstalled it and called support. They talked me through reinstalling it. The same issue happened and they were not able to fix it. After many hours invested in trying to make it work right, it just didn't work on my laptop for some reason. They refused to refund my money. I was a repeat customer, but am not a fan.

description: We have been with Carbonite since they started. The offer Peace of mind. If you have ever crashed without back-up you would know what a disaster that is. We back up all our PC and our NAS continuously.
author: Kim T.
pros: Carbonite is easy to set up on each machine. We can select what we want backed up. Restore can be done in a couple ways.
cons: Occasionally it will give you a message that something is not being backed up and 99% of the time if you check you will find it was being backed up anyway. Just little glitches like that.

author: Guy G.
pros: High value for money. Great back up schedule. Ease to restore files.
cons: Challenge to remove backed up files you delete from machine. Some times too many files are restored.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The tool does exactly what you need it to do - back up your hard drive to the cloud with ease!
cons: None - software is fantastic and has saved me once during a hard drive failure!
author_profession: Head Of Analytics / Data Science, Professional Project Management + Internal Audit

review_title: I can get support to help me
description: it a saturday so i asume the level 3 tech are not working and no one can hepl me reinstall the sofware...so right now my last back up was 10/31/18 and its 3/23/19 ...ouch
author: Lee G.
pros: that it backs up my files on my computer?
cons: its does not work, the software stops working and does not back up my computer. the last time it backed up my computer was Oct 31 and it March 23. so I call support spend an hour trying to get them to reinstall the software. and the said i need a level 3 tech to do so. so they are going to call me back in the mean time the software is off line right now ... this is crazy.

description: The software was quick and easy to install, and the first backup doesn't take too long. Once you have it set up, there's a small green dot beside everything to let you know it's safely backed up by Carbonite. Wonderful software.
author: Travis P.
pros: By far - what I most like about Carbonite is once it's installed, you pretty much forget about it. Lots of backup software requires frequent attention, but Carbonite just does its thing without bothering you.
cons: There's nothing negative to say about Carbonite.
